originalAlarm.description: The value written to `&codec_hw_write` is never used.

presenceCondition:
(and (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 false)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 true)
     (= CONFIG_I2C true)
     (= CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 false))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_DA7210 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_I2C () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_SND_FSI_DA7210 () Bool
  true)
reasoning: PC contains FSI_AK4642=T AND SOC_AK4642=F which is contradictory: #ifdef CONFIG_SND_FSI_AK4642 at L1 defines CONFIG_SND_SOC_AK4642 at L2, so FSI_AK4642=T always implies SOC_AK4642 is defined. Additionally FSI_DA7210=T AND SOC_DA7210=F is also contradictory (L5-6 define SOC_DA7210 when FSI_DA7210=T). This alarm arises from an infeasible configuration and is a false positive.
